packet_title: Assessment Evidence Packet Lite — Local History Source Paragraph
assignment: Write one paragraph explaining why a local newspaper source is stronger evidence than a social post for a town event.
learning_target: Explain source reliability using evidence, not just preference.

rules: AI may help brainstorm questions and define unfamiliar words.
AI may not write the final paragraph.
Learner must list any AI or human help used.

evidence.learner_claim: I can explain why the newspaper report is stronger because it names the meeting date, quotes an official notice, and can be checked against the town website.
process_trace: First draft said the newspaper was better because it sounded professional.
Learner compared the newspaper claim against the town notice.
Learner revised the paragraph to mention date, named source, and checkable record.
revision_or_misconception_log: Misconception: polished writing automatically means a source is reliable.
Revision: reliability depends on traceable evidence and checkable claims.
evidence.transfer_check: Learner must pick one new claim from a school newsletter and name the primary source that would confirm it.
source_or_tool_notes: Used AI to define primary source and secondary source.
Checked the fictional town notice provided by teacher.

notes: Ask learner to explain the difference between a source sounding credible and a source being checkable.
Do not use this packet as AI-detection evidence. Use it as learning evidence.
human_review.next_action: Have learner complete the transfer check before marking the learning target met.
